author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

metadata.author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon

title: Canon XVII.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Outlying or rural parishes shall in every province remain subject to the bishops who now have jurisdiction over them, particularly if the bishops have peaceably and continuously governed them for the space of thirty years.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if within thirty years there has been, or is, any dispute concerning them, it is lawful for those who hold themselves aggrieved to bring their cause before the synod of the province.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And if any one be wronged by his metropolitan, let the matter be decided by the exarch of the diocese or by the throne of Constantinople, as aforesaid.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And if any city has been, or shall hereafter be newly erected by imperial authority, let the order of the ecclesiastical parishes follow the political and municipal example.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon XVII.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Village and rural parishes if they have been possessed for thirty years, they shall so continue.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if within that time, the matter shall be subject to adjudication.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if by the command of the Emperor a city be renewed, the order of ecclesiastical parishes shall follow the civil and public forms.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The adjective ἐγχωρίους is probably synonymous with ἀγροικικάς (“rusticas,” Prisca), although Dionysius and Isidorian take in as “situated on estates,” cf.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Opusc., ii., 109.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: It was conceivable that some such outlying districts might form, ecclesiastically, a border-land, it might not be easy to assign them definitively to this or that bishopric.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: In such a case, says the Council, if the bishop who is now in possession of these rural churches can show a prescription of thirty years in favour of his see, let them remain undisturbed in his obedience.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: (Here ἀβιάστως may be illustrated from βιασάμενος in Eph. viij. and for the use of οἰκονομεῖν see I.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Const., ij.)

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But the border-land might be the “debate-able” land: the two neighbour bishops might dispute as to the right to tend these “sheep in the wilderness;” as we read in Cod.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Afric., 117, “multæ controversiæ postea inter episcopos de diœcesibus ortæ sunt, et oriuntur” (see on I.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Const., ij.); as archbishop Thomas of York, and Remigius of Dorchester, were at issue for years “with reference to Lindsey” (Raine, Fasti Eborac., i.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Accordingly, the canon provides that if such a contest had arisen within the thirty years, or should thereafter arise, the prelate who considered himself wronged might appeal to the provincial synod.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: If he should be aggrieved at the decision of his metropolitan in synod, he might apply for redress to the eparch (or prefect, a substitute for exarch) of the “diocese,” or to the see of Constantinople (in the manner provided by canon ix.).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: It is curious “that in Russia all the sees are divided into eparchies of the first, second, and third class” (Neale, Essays on Liturgiology, p.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars II., Causa XVI., Quæst. iii., can. j., in Isidore Mercator’s version.
